CSCO.rationale: CSCO trades at 26.8x earnings — upper-middle of its 1-year range — for only modest single-digit sequential revenue growth, and the stock has stalled after a 29% one-year run, making risk-reward slightly unfavorable near term.
key_factors: PE diluted at 26.8x sits in the upper portion of its 1y range (20.6–29.3), pricing in growth that quarterly data does not yet support
Quarterly revenue growth is modest: $13.99B → $14.15B → $14.67B (~3-4% Q/Q), with EPS $0.61 → $0.62 → $0.64
Stock flat over 3 months (-0.2%) after +29% 1-year return, suggesting momentum has stalled and easy gains are behind
Positive secular tailwinds in co-packaged optics (26.53% CAGR to 2032) and data center fabric markets provide some downside support

sources_cited.excerpt: Co-packaged Optics Market Size to Surpass USD 124.15 Billion by 2032, at 26.53% CAGR

DIS.rationale: DIS has de-rated sharply to 16.5x PE — near the floor of its 1y range — while EPS momentum is accelerating (1.40→1.81→2.92), making the risk/reward mildly attractive despite legal overhang and competitive noise.
key_factors: PE diluted at 16.5x, near the bottom of its 1y range of 16.1–43.2x, a dramatic de-rating from highs
EPS improving sharply over 3 quarters: 1.40 → 1.81 → 2.92, showing strong earnings acceleration
Stock down 6.9% over 3 months despite improving fundamentals, creating a potential disconnect
Rosen Law Firm investigation into Disney's children's privacy rights (Sept 14 & 21) adds legal/regulatory risk
Operating margin of 18.8% and net margin of 13.3% indicate solid profitability supporting the valuation floor

sources_cited.excerpt: Rosen Law Firm Investigates Disney's Potential Violation of Children's Privacy Rights
Rosen Law Firm Announces Investigation into Disney's Potential Violation Of Children's Privacy Rights

V.rationale: Visa's best-in-class margins and accelerating sequential revenue growth, combined with a modest 3-month pullback, make it a mild long; conviction is tempered by unavailable valuation ratios limiting relative cheapness assessment.
key_factors: Exceptional profitability: operating_margin=0.622, net_margin=0.522, roe=0.525 — among the highest in corporate America
Sequential revenue growth accelerating: 9.51B (Dec'24) → 9.59B (Mar'25) → 10.17B (Jun'25), a 6.1% sequential jump
3-month pullback of -3.6% from ~$351 to $341.38 after a strong +24.1% 1-year run, offering a modest better entry
News sentiment mostly positive (Warren Buffett buy thesis, Dow stock to soar) with one minor negative (shareholder investigation Sep 17)

sources_cited.excerpt: 1 Warren Buffett Quote That Makes Me Excited to Buy Visa Stock

KO.rationale: KO's valuation has compressed to the low end of its one-year range while fundamentals keep improving sequentially, making the risk-reward mildly attractive for a high-quality defensive name.
key_factors: PE compressed to 23.5x, at the very bottom of its 1-year range (23.2–30.0), while ROE remains 40.4% and net margin 25.9%
Sequential quarterly improvement: Q1 EPS $0.77 → Q2 EPS $0.88, revenue $11.13B → $12.54B
Stock down 7.6% over past year and 6.5% over 3 months, creating a pullback entry in a defensive dividend king
News sentiment net positive (most days +1.00) with multiple 'buy now' and dividend-income articles in late September

WMT.rationale: At 42.6x diluted PE — the top of its 1-year range — WMT prices in perfection for a 2.8% net-margin business after a 29% one-year run, leaving risk-reward unattractive despite solid sequential EPS improvement.
key_factors: PE diluted at 42.575x, near the top of its 1y range of 28.8–43.1, after a +29.2% 1-year price run
Net margin of just 2.8% and operating margin of 4.3% provide little earnings cushion at this multiple
Q2 EPS improved to $0.88 from $0.56 prior quarter and revenue grew to $177.4B from $165.6B — fundamentals are decent but already reflected
News flow is generic market commentary with no WMT-specific catalysts; sentiment mildly positive but thin

TRV.rationale: Reasonable valuation (12.2x PE, 17.8% ROE) with recovering earnings and positive momentum makes TRV a mild long, though Q1 earnings volatility caps conviction.
key_factors: PE at 12.2x sits in lower-middle of 1y range (10.9-15.3x), with solid 17.8% ROE and 11% net margin supporting fair-to-attractive valuation
Q2 2025 EPS rebounded to 6.53 after weak Q1 of 1.70, showing earnings volatility but underlying resilience
Positive 1y price momentum of +18.3% with recent stability in 270-280 range, not overextended
Recent news sentiment turned positive (+1.00 on 2025-09-16 AI/dividend framing), consistent with improving sentiment trend since March 2025

PG.rationale: High-quality consumer staples franchise trading near the low end of its valuation range after an 11.5% one-year drawdown, but recent quarterly revenue and EPS softness cap the upside and keep conviction moderate.
key_factors: PE at 24.2x near the bottom of its 1y range (23.7–30.8), a meaningful de-rating for a 29.7% ROE franchise
1-year price return of -11.5% has compressed the stock to a more attractive entry for a defensive consumer staple
Recent quarterly revenue softness: Q3 FY25 rev=19.78B vs 21.88B and 21.74B in prior two quarters, with EPS=1.54 the lowest in the recent sequence
News framing as a 'recession-proof dividend king' and 'a steal' suggests sentiment is turning constructive at the lows

sources_cited.excerpt: Why These 2 Recession-Proof Dividend Kings Are a Steal Right Now

GS.rationale: GS trades at a reasonable 16.3x PE with strong margins, but after a 60% one-year run and a sequential EPS decline in Q2, the risk/reward is only mildly attractive.
key_factors: PE diluted 16.3x sits in upper half of 1y range (11.4–18.3), reasonable but not cheap after +59.7% 1y price run
Q2 2025 EPS declined sequentially to 10.91 from Q1's 14.12, signaling some earnings softness
Strong profitability: net margin 27.7%, operating margin 35.3%, but ROE only 12.5% is modest for the franchise
Price momentum strong (+11.2% in 3 months) but news flow is largely GS-irrelevant with neutral sentiment

AXP.rationale: Solid fundamental momentum (rising EPS and revenue, 31% ROE) is largely priced in at 22.9x PE near the top of its recent range, making the risk-adjusted reward only mildly positive.
key_factors: PE at 22.9x near top of 1y range (16.5–23.6), limiting valuation cushion after +22.5% 1y rally
Strong sequential EPS growth: Q2'25 $4.08 vs Q1'25 $3.64 vs Q4'24 $3.04, with revenue rising to $10.32B
High returns: ROE 31.4%, operating margin 32.4%, net margin 25.6% — quality franchise compounding
Debt-to-equity at 1.80 with $58.2B total debt against $32.3B equity adds balance-sheet risk if credit cycle turns

CRM.rationale: Valuation compression to the low end of its one-year PE range, combined with improving quarterly fundamentals and a strengthening balance sheet, makes the risk-reward mildly attractive after a 14% one-year drawdown.
key_factors: PE compressed to 37.3x, bottom of 1y range (36.4–77.7x) after -14.3% 1y price decline
Q2 revenue recovered to 10.24B and EPS improved to 1.96 from 1.59 prior quarter
Strong balance sheet: debt/equity=0.138, gross margin=77.2%, operating margin=19%
News sentiment improving in late September (+0.50 to +1.00 scores) with 'comeback' narrative building

sources_cited.excerpt: revenue=10.24B, eps_diluted=1.96 (up from 9.83B rev, 1.59 eps prior quarter)
2025-09-25: +1.00, 2025-09-30: +1.00; 'Downgraded but Not Done: 3 Stocks Ready for a Market Comeback'

HD.rationale: HD trades at a mid-range 27.5x PE after pulling back ~4% from recent highs, with sequentially improving EPS and positive housing-rate tailwinds offering mild upside against limited downside risk.
key_factors: PE diluted 27.5x sits mid-range of its 1y band (22.5–29.3), not expensive after a ~4% pullback from the 423 peak
Q1 FY2025 EPS improved to $3.45 from $3.01 prior quarter, with revenue up to $39.86B from $39.70B — sequential growth despite flat YoY
News sentiment net positive (mostly +1.00 days) with falling mortgage rates cited as a potential housing tailwind
1-year return of just +1.4% suggests range-bound stock with limited downside after consolidation

sources_cited.excerpt: Plunging Mortgage Rates Could Light Up These 9 Stocks

MCD.rationale: High-quality franchise with best-in-class margins and steady mid-single-digit growth trading at a reasonable ~25x after a flat year and recent pullback offers mild risk-adjusted upside with limited downside.
key_factors: Strong margins: operating_margin=0.458, net_margin=0.322 — best-in-class franchise economics
Steady growth: Q2'25 rev=6.84B vs Q2'24 rev=6.39B (~7% YoY), EPS 3.14 vs 2.80 (~12% YoY)
Valuation reasonable at ~25x earnings per Sept 19 news article, within 1y range pe_diluted 0..28.2
Stock flat over 1y (ret_1y=+0.1%) and pulled back ~4% from early-Sept highs near 316 to 303.89, offering a better entry
News sentiment net positive (multiple +1.00 scores), articles framing MCD as dividend/growth value play

sources_cited.excerpt: McDonald's Stock Still Offers Upside Despite Trading at 25x Earnings

HON.rationale: A 12% pullback has brought HON to a reasonable 22.8x PE for a high-ROE (31.7%), high-margin (20.1% op) industrial with a fresh dividend hike, but flat-to-declining revenue and elevated leverage limit the reward-to-risk skew.
key_factors: Stock pulled back 12% in 3 months to $198.40, with PE at 22.8x mid-range vs 1y band of 19.8–26.0x — reasonable for 31.7% ROE and 20.1% operating margin
EPS growing despite revenue decline: Q2'25 EPS $2.45 vs $2.36 YoY while revenue fell to $9.32B from $9.58B, signaling margin resilience
Dividend increase announced Sept 26 signals management confidence in cash-flow durability
Elevated leverage with debt/equity at 1.54x ($30.24B debt vs $16.65B equity) and soft top-line trend cap upside

sources_cited.excerpt: HONEYWELL TO INCREASE DIVIDEND EFFECTIVE FOURTH QUARTER 2025

IBM.rationale: At 45.9x earnings near the top of its 1-year range, IBM's valuation has outrun its modest 9% margins and unproven AI/quantum revenue contribution, making the risk of multiple compression outweigh the momentum-driven reward over the next year.
key_factors: PE diluted at 45.9x, near top of 1-year range (23.3–50.9), demanding significant growth justification
Net margin only 9.1% and operating margin 9.8% — thin profitability for a 46x multiple
Stock rallied ~17% in 6 weeks (241→282) on AI/quantum sentiment, but Q2 revenue 16.98B shows modest sequential growth
News sentiment consistently positive (+0.5 to +1.0) with AI/quantum narrative, but momentum may be front-loaded at these valuations

JNJ.rationale: Reasonable valuation (19.8x PE mid-range), solid margins, and positive pipeline news support a mild long, but the recent +19.2% 3-month rally and lingering litigation overhang cap conviction.
key_factors: PE at 19.8x sits mid-range of its 1y band (10.2–29.0), reasonable for 25% net margin and 29% ROE
Q2 2025 revenue grew to 23.74B from 21.89B in Q1, showing sequential top-line acceleration
Tecvayli-Darzalex 100% response in newly diagnosed myeloma (Sep 19) and JNJ-1900 Phase 1 data (Sep 29) strengthen pipeline outlook
Stock has rallied +19.2% in 3 months, limiting near-term upside; Kenvue litigation article (Sep 22) flags lingering talc/regulatory risk

sources_cited.excerpt: Johnson & Johnson's Tecvayli-Darzalex Combo Shows 100% Response In Newly Diagnosed Myeloma Patients

Kenvue Stock Pressure Highlights Litigation and Regulatory Risk Premium

VZ.rationale: A ~6% dividend yield backed by steady mid-single-digit revenue and EPS growth offers a reasonable risk-adjusted return, but PE near the top of its recent range and lack of strong catalysts cap upside.
key_factors: Dividend yield ~6%+ provides substantial income cushion with steady cash flows (multiple news articles cite 6%+ yield)
Revenue growing modestly: Q2'25 rev=34.50B vs Q2'24 rev=32.80B (~5% YoY), EPS up from 1.09 to 1.18 (~8% YoY)
PE at 18.1x near top of 1-year range (14.9-19.1), limiting multiple-expansion upside
Stock flat over past year (ret_1y=-2.1%) with narrow 43-45 trading band, suggesting market already prices in the dividend story
Positive product catalyst: first carrier to offer AI-powered Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses (2025-09-25)

sources_cited.excerpt: With a Dividend Yield of More Than 6%, Is Verizon Stock a Buy?
Verizon to be the first carrier to offer the new AI-powered Meta Ray-Ban Display glasses

MRK.rationale: Beaten-down pharma trading at 12.8x earnings with strong margins and improving news flow offers favorable risk-reward, though flat revenue and declining quarterly EPS cap conviction.
key_factors: PE at 12.8x near the low end of its 1y range (10.7–21.7), with 25.8% net margin and 33.5% ROE — cheap for this profitability
Stock down 26.2% over the past year but bounced +6.8% on 9/30 to $83.93, suggesting capitulation and potential reversal
Positive catalysts: FDA cleared one-minute cancer shot (9/22), Winrevair 76% risk reduction data (9/30), Verona Pharma acquisition approved (9/24)
Q2 EPS declined to $1.76 from $2.01 prior quarter and revenue growth is flat (~$15.5–15.8B), limiting upside

sources_cited.excerpt: FDA Clears Merck's One-Minute Cancer Shot
Merck's Winrevair Cuts Clinical Worsening Risk By 76% Among Hypertension Patients
Shareholders of Verona Pharma Approve Proposed Acquisition by Merck

CVX.rationale: CVX trades near the top of its recent valuation range on deteriorating earnings, with oil below $70 likely to keep pressure on margins — the reward does not adequately compensate for the earnings-decline risk.
key_factors: PE at 19.43x is near the top of its 1y range (13.8–20.2) while EPS is declining: Q2 EPS 1.45 vs Q1 2.00 vs Q4'24 1.84
Sequential revenue declining: Q2 rev 44.82B vs Q1 47.61B, with oil below $70/barrel pressuring forward margins
Net margin only 7.1% and ROE 9.4% — weak returns for a stock trading at premium-to-range valuation
News sentiment consistently positive (+1.00 on most days) but largely dividend/yield-driven, not earnings-driven

sources_cited.excerpt: Should You Buy Chevron Stock With Oil Prices Below $70 a Barrel?

MSFT.rationale: Exceptional franchise quality and accelerating quarterly growth support a premium, but valuation at the top of its own 1-year range (PE 37.97x vs 29.3–40.6x) limits risk-adjusted upside to a mild long lean.
key_factors: Valuation near top of 1y range: pe_diluted=37.97x vs 29.3–40.6x range, ev_to_ebit=29.68x vs 23.1–32.3x range — limited margin of safety
Strong sequential growth: quarterly revenue 65.58B→70.07B→76.44B and EPS 3.30→3.46→3.65 over last three quarters
Fortress balance sheet: cash=94.56B vs total_debt=43.15B, debt_to_equity=0.126, net_margin=36.1%, operating_margin=45.6%
Positive sentiment trend: net news sentiment +0.70 to +0.83 on Sep 29–30, stock at recent high close=517.95 with 1y return +21.0%

NKE.rationale: Nike's earnings power is collapsing (quarterly EPS fell from 0.78 to 0.14) while the stock trades at 32x trailing earnings near the top of its yearly range — the market has not yet fully priced the deterioration, and no turnaround catalyst is evident.
key_factors: Quarterly EPS collapsing: 0.78 → 0.54 → 0.14, with latest quarter annualized EPS ~0.56 implying a ~125x PE on run-rate earnings
PE of 32.3x sits near the top of its 1y range (17.5–36.7) despite operating margin at just 8.4% and gross margin at 42.7% — both well below Nike's historical norms
Revenue declining sequentially: 12.35B → 11.27B → 11.10B across the last three quarters, showing no inflection
News sentiment net negative (-0.50 on 9/30, -0.33 on 9/29) with Kim Kardashian partnership framed as a competitive response to Lululemon rather than organic innovation
Stock already down 22% over 1 year, providing some mean-reversion cushion but no fundamental catalyst yet visible

sources_cited.excerpt: Nike Is Partnering With Kim Kardashian to Take on Lululemon. Can It Make the Stock a Winner?

AMZN.rationale: AMZN trades near the bottom of its own one-year PE range (33.6x vs 30.3–52.1) while earnings and margins are still expanding sequentially, making the post-pullback entry a favorable risk/reward.
key_factors: PE diluted at 33.6x, near the low end of its 1-year range (30.3–52.1), while EPS is growing sequentially (Q1 1.59 → Q2 1.68)
Operating margin 11.4% and net margin 10.5% with ROE 21.2% show durable profitability expansion
Stock pulled back ~7.8% from early-September high of 238.24 to 219.57, improving entry after a flat 3-month period
Low leverage (debt/equity 0.168) limits balance-sheet risk; news sentiment net positive at +0.40 on 9/29–9/30

AMGN.rationale: Declining EPS trajectory (~50% drop in three quarters) combined with extreme leverage (D/E=8.0x) and a still-premium 36x PE outweigh decent revenue growth and near-range-low valuation, making risk-adjusted reward mildly unattractive.
key_factors: EPS declining sharply across recent quarters: $5.22 (Q3'24) → $3.20 (Q1'25) → $2.65 (Q2'25), ~50% drop in three quarters despite revenue growth
Extreme leverage: debt_to_equity=8.02x with total_debt=$56.2B vs total_equity=$7.43B, constraining financial flexibility
PE at 36.2x near low end of 1y range (33.1–56.8) but still a premium multiple for deteriorating earnings
Revenue growth of ~9.4% YoY ($9.18B vs $8.39B) and positive news sentiment (+1.00 on multiple recent days) provide partial offset
Stock already down -12.5% over past year, pricing in some bad news

UNH.rationale: UNH trades at a historically depressed 14.2x PE with strong franchise metrics (22% ROE, 88% gross margin) and a price recovery underway, but the sharp sequential EPS decline from $6.85 to $3.74 caps conviction as margin compression remains unresolved.
key_factors: PE diluted at 14.2x vs 1y range of 9.5–38.7x — near historical floor, pricing in worst-case scenario
Q2 2025 EPS collapsed to $3.74 from $6.85 in Q1 — margin compression is the core risk
3-month price recovery of +12.3% from ~$300 lows with positive news sentiment suggests turnaround narrative gaining traction
ROE of 22% and gross margin of 88.2% confirm franchise quality remains intact despite near-term earnings pressure

sources_cited.excerpt: 2025-09-30 — 1 Reason This Healthcare Stock's Turnaround Is on the Horizon

NVDA.rationale: Extraordinary growth and margins at a mid-range relative valuation support continued upside, but the stock's 53.7% one-year run, 52-week-high price, and uniformly bullish sentiment cap the risk-adjusted reward to a mild long.
key_factors: PE diluted at 52.9x sits mid-range of its own 1y band (32.1–70.0x), reasonable given EPS grew 42% QoQ ($0.76→$1.08)
Exceptional profitability: net margin 52.4%, operating margin 58.1%, ROE 86.5%, with near-zero leverage (D/E=0.085)
Revenue accelerating sequentially: $44.06B→$46.74B (+6.1% QoQ), backed by large AI infrastructure deals (CoreWeave $14.2B Meta contract)
Stock at 52-week high after +53.7% 1y return with uniformly bullish sentiment (avg +0.55 daily), creating contrarian and valuation-stretch risk
Geopolitical risk escalating: Huawei chip competition in China cited as deepening threat

sources_cited.excerpt: CoreWeave Deepens AI Moat With $14.2B Meta Deal After OpenAI Expansion
Nvidia's China Nightmare Deepens—Huawei's Chip Army Is Coming

MMM.rationale: Modest sequential revenue growth and a mid-range PE are offset by elevated leverage (D/E 2.98) and inconsistent EPS, making the risk-reward only mildly positive with no clear catalyst.
key_factors: Sequential revenue growth: Q4'24 6.01B → Q1'25 5.95B → Q2'25 6.34B shows modest but consistent top-line recovery post-spinoff
PE at 19.4x sits mid-range of its 1y band (16.7–20.5), neither cheap nor expensive — limited valuation edge
High leverage with debt_to_equity=2.98 and total_debt=13.15B vs equity of 4.35B constrains financial flexibility and adds downside risk
EPS volatile: Q1'25 EPS of 2.04 dropped to 1.34 in Q2'25, making earnings quality and trajectory uncertain
End-market news mildly positive (PPE market CAGR 6.04%, packaging tape growth) but no company-specific catalysts

sources_cited.excerpt: PPE Market to Grow USD 137.69 Billion by 2032, at a CAGR of 6.04%

BA.rationale: FAA easing of 737 Max production restrictions is a genuine near-term catalyst that could unlock volume-driven margin recovery, but deeply negative margins, a stressed balance sheet with negative equity, and a 38% prior-year rally temper the reward-to-risk profile.
key_factors: FAA set to ease 737 Max production restrictions and let Boeing regain partial certification authority (Sept 26 news, sentiment +1.00 n=5) — the dominant catalyst for production ramp and margin recovery
Still deeply unprofitable: TTM net margin -14.2%, gross margin -0.3%, Q2 2025 EPS -0.92 widened from Q1's -0.16 despite revenue rising to 22.75B
Balance sheet severely stressed: total debt 53.14B vs negative equity of -3.30B, limiting financial flexibility
Stock pulled back ~9% from late-August highs (~236 to 215.83) after +38.1% 1-year rally, offering a better entry point into the recovery thesis

JPM.rationale: Stock has returned +49.8% in a year almost entirely through PE multiple expansion (11x→16.8x) while YoY earnings declined, leaving limited upside at a full valuation despite sequentially improving fundamentals and strong franchise margins.
key_factors: PE at top of 1y range: pe_diluted=16.8x vs 11.0-16.8x range, after +49.8% 1-year price run-up driven largely by multiple expansion
YoY fundamentals declining: Q2 2025 revenue $44.91B vs Q2 2024 $50.20B, EPS $5.24 vs $6.12 — stock gains not supported by earnings growth
Sequential improvement visible: EPS rose from $4.37 (Q3 2024) to $5.07 (Q1 2025) to $5.24 (Q2 2025), suggesting trough may be behind
Strong franchise quality: net_margin=0.31, roe=0.155, operating_margin=0.398 support a floor but don't justify further multiple expansion
News sentiment mildly positive: dividend growth headlines (+0.67 on 2025-09-25) offset by fraud/insider-selling noise

AAPL.rationale: AAPL trades at 38.3x earnings with decelerating quarterly revenue and EPS, and after a +19.9% three-month rally the risk-reward skews modestly negative as the multiple prices in growth that the fundamentals don't yet confirm.
key_factors: PE at 38.3x sits in upper-middle of its 1y range (27.2–42.6) while quarterly EPS declined sequentially from 1.65 to 1.57 and revenue slipped from 95.36B to 94.04B
Stock has rallied +19.9% in 3 months to 254.63, pricing in much of the iPhone 17 holiday optimism
Operating margin remains strong at 31.9% and gross margin 46.7%, supporting franchise quality but not enough to justify the multiple expansion
News sentiment improving (+0.56 on Sep 30) with iPhone 17 boost narrative, but sentiment is a lagging indicator after a 20% run

sources_cited.excerpt: Apple Stock Positioned for Holiday Gains With iPhone 17 Boost

SHW.rationale: A ~10% pullback has brought SHW's PE to the lower-middle of its 1-year range while sequential fundamentals improve and sentiment stays positive, but valuation remains full and the downtrend is a caution flag.
key_factors: PE diluted at 34.2x, in lower-middle of 1y range (29.7–39.7) after a -9.5% 1-year price decline
Q2 2025 revenue 6.31B and EPS 3.00, up from Q1's 5.31B and 2.00 — improving sequential trajectory
Exceptional ROE of 57.8% supports quality thesis, but debt/equity of 2.04 adds balance sheet risk
News sentiment predominantly positive (10 of 14 observations at +1.00)

sources_cited.excerpt: net sentiment +1.00, predominantly positive across 14 observations

CAT.rationale: CAT trades at the top of its 1-year PE range (22.9x) after a 20% 3-month rally, but earnings are softening (Q2 EPS 4.62 vs Q4 5.78), making the risk of valuation mean-reversion outweigh the high-quality business fundamentals.
key_factors: PE at top of 1y range: 22.9x vs 12.4-22.9 range, driven by price not earnings
Sharp 3-month rally of +19.8% to 477.15, with September alone adding ~14%
Earnings softening: Q2 2025 EPS 4.62 down from Q4 2024 EPS 5.78
Strong but already-priced business quality: ROE 55%, operating margin 19.2%
